title: About Bambos – Bambos
text_excerpt: About Bambos – Bambos Home About News Southgate and Wood Green Parliament Campaigns PETITION: Use of Shops for Illegal Activities Local Economy Health Education International Issues Community Safety Southgate and Wood Green Wins My Newsletter Contact Search Menu Menu About Bambos ___ I am the Labour MP for Southgate and Wood Green. I was first elected as the Labour MP for Enfield Southgate in June 2017 and re-elected in December 2019. I am the first MP elected for the new constituency of Southgate and Wood Green at the 2024 General Election. Before my parliamentary career began, I was a solicitor and worked for Hackney Council on their housing litigation team for 16 years. I was the first MP whose parents are both from Cyprus. I was born in London and raised in Bowes Park. I love Enfield – I grew up here and many of my friends and family live in the area. As a child, I went to St Michael at Bowes Junior school, where I am now a school governor. I was a councillor for Palmers Green ward for 24 years – the place that has been my home for most of my life. Before entering Parliament, I was Enfield Council’s cabinet member for Leisure, Culture, Localism and Young People. I have previously sat on Labour’s frontbench as the Shadow Immigration Minister and Shadow Foreign Office Minister for the Middle East and North Africa. I will always speak up for Southgate and Wood Green in Parliament and campaign on the issues that matter to you. 55743 Constituency contacts 455 Parliamentary Questions asked 8717 Letters to Government Ministers 458 Speeches in parliament Together We Are Stronger Standing up for Southgate and Wood Green Representing You As your MP, I am always raising the issues that matter to you in Parliament. Every week, I question and challenge the Government and Ministers on their work and make sure the views of the people of Southgate and Wood Green are brought to the heart of politics. My record in Parliament can be found here . 55743 Constituency contacts 455 Parliamentary Questions asked 8717 Letters to Government Ministers 458 Speeches in Parliament Address Westminster address: House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA Facebook Twitter Instagram Contact Email bambos.charalambous.mp @parliament.uk Telephone Constituency Office 0208 882 0088 Scroll to top
